Subject: Renewal Call
Description:
04-13-2026 Fredrik Scheike_ The meeting was an initial renewal discussion for Allo’s Kayako subscription where the Trilogy renewals manager introduced himself, confirmed call recording, explained Trilogy’s role in supporting multiple product lines, and outlined the agenda covering renewal-term and price-lock options, Standard versus Platinum success plans, and Prime program credits. The vendor confirmed terms remain unchanged from last year, including the 60-day cancellation window before the renewal date of August 28, noted recently added AI-driven Kayako features, and reviewed last year’s spend and typical lift rates to frame one-, three-, and five-year price-lock options with annual payment scheduling.

Allo attendees identified themselves as IT administrators who manage Kayako from a technical/admin perspective and explained they inherited the product roughly five years ago. Paul said he could not commit to multi-year terms without consulting his forecasting and manager because his forecasting horizon is about 16–18 months. Fredrik clarified that quotes are non-binding until signed, offered to produce a three-year quote and a feature/service-level comparison, and proposed follow-up dates in May. The group agreed to reconvene on May 25, Fredrik offered to send the Renewals portal link and collateral after the call, and the vendor restated the final decision deadline of June 28.

Subject: Renewal Call
Description:
04-13-2026 Fredrik Scheike: The meeting reviewed the upcoming Kayako renewal, current product usage, and consolidation planning across a 21-company corporate group. Piotr confirmed he is covering Joe’s responsibilities during Joe’s transition toward retirement and that he has downloaded a support data dump for backup and compliance; the broader consolidation decision is expected around August. The group’s operational notes included daily Kayako use, intermittent resolvable issues, and an SLA attainment of about 91%, plus limited AI adoption and use of an out-of-system knowledge base.

Discussion of commercial terms clarified that Kayako requires a minimum 12-month contract and also offers three- and five-year terms, with multi-year price locks avoiding planned increases but monthly contracts unavailable. The buyer signaled a preference for a 12-month renewal and set an internal decision deadline of 17 May for a July renewal. Fredrik described Kayako’s Prime program and available credits (including an AWS cost optimizer) and offered follow-up materials. The parties agreed to a 15-minute follow-up check-in proposed for Monday, May 4 at 14:00 UK time, and the seller committed to schedule the call, update recipient emails, send the 12-month quote, and provide a written summary.

Subject: Check-in Call
Description:
MEETING SUMMARY
* Meeting date and time: 04/09/2026
* Meeting attendees:
* Sebastian Desand (IgniteTech / Khoros – Renewals)
* Roxanne Sookdeo-Macias (Customer – Procurement / Senior Sourcing Specialist)
* Kerrie Thometz (Customer – Knowledge Specialist / Admin)
* Meeting length: ~26 minutes
Talking points:
Purpose of call – early renewal check-in
* Sebastian positioned the call as an early-stage renewal discussion to avoid last-minute pressure.
* Objective: review usage, discuss plans, and align on renewal process.
Current usage of Khoros
* Primary use case: knowledge base (not community features)
* Used for:
* Student policy handbook
* Self-service resources
* Public-facing compliance content (accreditation/government)
* Platform is mission-critical across entire student lifecycle (enrollment → graduation).
Limited use of community features
* Forums and community elements are minimally used.
* Customer has moved community use to another platform.
Customer challenges
* High turnover at IgniteTech:
* “about every three or four months we get a new person”
* Lack of internal technical resources:
* No engineering support for customization
* Outdated UI:
* “hasn’t been changed in 10 years”
* Overall sentiment: functionality is stable, but experience and support continuity are issues.
Aurora migration discussion
* Customer is aware of Aurora but has blockers:
* Missing feature: promoted search not yet available
* No internal resources to support migration
* Continuous operations make upgrades difficult (“changing a tire while the bus is moving”)
Vendor incentive for migration
* Sebastian highlighted:
* Migration fee (~$100K) currently waived for renewing customers
* Migration can be scheduled later, not immediately at renewal.
Operational constraints
* Customer operates continuous enrollment model:
* No downtime windows
* Monthly student intake creates constant platform usage
* Any migration requires careful timing and planning.
Interest in AI and future capabilities
* Customer expressed interest in:
* AI-powered search and agents
* Cross-platform search experience for students
* Requested:
* Demo of Aurora AI capabilities
* Sandbox/test environment
Renewal intent
* Procurement confirmed:
* “I believe the plan is to renew”
* No resistance to renewal at this stage.
Procurement and contracting process
* Requirements before signing:
* Legal review
* Security risk assessment questionnaire
* Internal approvals
* Procurement system now handled via e-procurement platform (not email)
MSA / legal terms
* If terms unchanged → reuse existing MSA (faster)
* If new terms introduced → full legal review required
Commercial process
* Next step: formal renewal call with pricing/options (1-year vs multi-year)
* Quote will be generated after alignment on structure
Administrative updates
* Need to confirm:
* Billing details
* Signatory (has changed recently)
* Primary contact

INSIGHTS
* Renewal likelihood: Likely to renew (low risk short-term)
* Customer explicitly indicated intent to renew.
* No pricing or contractual pushback raised in this call.
* Key drivers of renewal:
* Platform is deeply embedded in student journey
* Switching cost likely high due to compliance/public content use
* Risk factors (medium-term):
* Poor experience with staff turnover at IgniteTech
* Product perceived as stagnant (UI, innovation gaps)
* Limited use of broader platform capabilities (reduced perceived value)
* Opportunity areas:
* Aurora migration (especially with fee waived)
* AI capabilities as a future value driver
* Improved relationship management via stable account coverage
* Constraints:
* Customer lacks technical resources → requires vendor-led support
* Continuous operations make migration complex

ACTIONS FROM THE MEETING
* Sebastian (Khoros):
* Arrange Aurora demo (via Marcelo)
* Provide sandbox/test environment
* Confirm MSA status (reuse vs new terms)
* Send follow-up summary and admin info request
* Prepare for formal renewal options call
* Roxanne (Customer):
* Provide billing details and signatory information
* Initiate legal review and security assessment process
* Coordinate internal stakeholders for demo
* Kerrie (Customer):
* Validate usage and requirements
* Participate in Aurora evaluation (demo/sandbox)

Subject: Check-in Call
Description:
Call on Teams; transcript taken manually.
---

1. Meeting Summary

* Meeting date and time: Not specified in transcript

* Meeting attendees:

* Tim Courtenay (Supplier – IgniteTech/DNN)
* Jerry Dudziak (Fidelis Care / Centene)
* Tim Trehy (Fidelis Care / Centene, Procurement)

* Meeting length: ~32 minutes

* Talking points:

1. Renewal intent and structure

* Customer stated: “*Obviously, we would like to renew*”
* Requested both **1-year and 2-year renewal options**, with expectation of flexibility on term length
* Supplier reiterated **2-year renewals are not permitted under policy**, only 1-year or 3-year

2. Pricing concerns and expectations

* Customer challenged pricing as “*on the unreasonable side*”
* Highlighted discrepancy between expected **25% increase vs actual ~45% increase**
* Supplier clarified:

* 45% increase tied to **Platinum success tier**
* 25% applies to **Standard success tier**
* Prior lower increase on Platinum was **not policy-compliant exception**

3. Strong negative customer sentiment toward supplier

* Customer stated: “*I know you guys are difficult to deal with… I don’t think that’s a reputation you want to have*”
* Described prior engagements as “*never been beneficial… just goes in circles*”

4. Major dissatisfaction with support and service delivery

* Ongoing technical issue lasted **~1 year to resolve**
* Customer stated:

* “*It took a full year to get somebody on a phone… once we did, it was solved*”
* “*Nothing’s as advertised*” regarding Platinum support
* Specific issues:

* Lack of access to promised **phone support**
* Over-reliance on **automated ticketing system**
* Tickets auto-closing and difficulty escalating
* Customer believes **Platinum services were not delivered as contracted**

5. Value perception and potential downgrade

* Customer questioning value of **Platinum tier**
* Considering **removal of enhanced support entirely**
* Statement: “*I don’t see any value in keeping it*”

6. Supplier positioning and constraints

* Strict renewal policies:

* No 2-year deals
* Price increases tied to acquisition model and profitability goals
* Emphasis on **multi-year (3-year) agreements** to mitigate increases
* Acknowledged support issues but separated from renewal discussion

7. Commercial requests from customer

* Request to:

* **Reduce license count** (7 → 6 licenses)
* **Reduce core count by 1**
* Requested **side-by-side 1-year and 3-year pricing**
* Requested **aggressive pricing** to justify 3-year commitment

8. Credits / compensation discussion

* Customer raised expectation of **credit for service failures**
* Supplier stated:

* Credits/claims handled separately
* **Not applied to renewal pricing**

9. Next steps alignment

* Supplier to provide:

* Updated quotes (1-year and 3-year)
* Pricing for **Standard vs Platinum**
* պարզ breakdown of entitlements and programs
* Timeline: **Late Monday or Tuesday delivery**

---

Insights

* Customer renewal likelihood: **At risk**

* While customer states intent to renew, sentiment is highly negative
* Strong dissatisfaction with support and value delivery
* Explicit openness to **moving away from the product** acknowledged by both parties

* Key renewal drivers:

* Significant **price reduction or justification**
* Clear improvement or explanation of **support model and delivery**
* Ability to demonstrate **real value from Platinum or justify downgrade**

* Key risks:

* Customer perceives **systemic failure in service delivery**
* Customer distrust in supplier commitments (“nothing’s as advertised”)
* Procurement pushing aggressively on **terms, pricing, and concessions**

* Policy conflicts (likely non-negotiable):

* 2-year renewal request (explicitly denied)
* Price increase resistance
* Potential expectation of **credits/discounts tied to past failures**

* Opportunity:

* Position **Standard tier downgrade** as cost-saving option
* Use **3-year deal to stabilize pricing**
* Rebuild trust via **clear entitlement communication**

---

2. Actions from the Meeting

* Tim Courtenay (Supplier)

* Provide **1-year and 3-year renewal quotes**
* Include **Standard vs Platinum pricing options**
* Reflect **reduced license count (6) and reduced core count**
* Share **clear breakdown of Platinum vs Standard entitlements**
* Provide **additional info on programs (e.g., Prime program)**
* Deliver by **late Monday / Tuesday**

* Tim Courtenay (Supplier – internal)

* Review **Platinum support delivery vs contractual commitments**
* Investigate **lack of phone support access**
* Align internally on **handling of credit/service failure discussions**

* Jerry Dudziak (Customer)

* Review provided quotes and entitlement breakdown
* Decide on **support tier (likely downgrade)**

* Tim Trehy (Customer – Procurement)

* Evaluate **1-year vs 3-year commercial options**
* Determine negotiation position and renewal strategy

---

3. Summary for Salesforce Opportunity Description field

Renewal call with Jerry Dudziak and Tim Trehy (Fidelis Care / Centene) to discuss DNN renewal. Customer confirmed intent to renew but raised significant concerns regarding pricing (45% increase vs expected 25%), strict renewal policies (rejection of 2-year term), and major dissatisfaction with support, including a year-long unresolved issue and failure to deliver Platinum support benefits. Customer is considering reducing licenses, cores, and downgrading support, and requires aggressive pricing to consider a 3-year agreement.

Actions:

* Supplier to provide 1-year and 3-year quotes with reduced licenses/cores and Standard vs Platinum pricing (by late Mon/Tues)
* Supplier to share clear entitlement breakdown and program details
* Customer to review quotes and decide on support tier
* Procurement to evaluate renewal approach

---

4. Sales next step summary

Provide updated renewal quotes (1-year and 3-year) and clear Standard vs Platinum comparison, along with supporting documentation to rebuild value perception. Required by early next week. Tim Courtenay

---

5. Probable Outcome

Probable Outcome: Undetermined

Probable Outcome Notes: Net risk is high. Customer has stated intent to renew but expressed strong dissatisfaction with support (“took a full year to resolve”) and value (“nothing’s as advertised”), and challenged pricing and policies. Procurement is pushing aggressively on terms and may consider alternatives if expectations are not met.

---

Hi Jerry, Tim,

Thanks for your time earlier.

To summarise the key points from our discussion:

* You confirmed your intent to renew DNN, while evaluating both 1-year and 3-year options
* You raised concerns around the proposed pricing, particularly the increase associated with the current support level
* We discussed our renewal policies, including the availability of 1-year and 3-year terms
* You shared detailed feedback on your experience with support over the past year, including the challenges in accessing timely assistance and the perceived gap between Platinum support commitments and delivery
* You are considering reducing your footprint (licenses and cores) and potentially moving away from the current support level based on value received

Next steps:

* Tim Courtenay: Provide updated 1-year and 3-year quotes reflecting reduced license and core requirements, along with Standard and Platinum options (by late Monday / Tuesday)
* Tim Courtenay: Share a clear breakdown of support entitlements and additional program details
* Jerry / Tim: Review the options and confirm preferred approach to renewal and support level

Please let me know if there’s anything we missed or if you’d like to add further context.

Happy to reconnect once you’ve had a chance to review the options.

Best regards,
Tim

Subject: Enterprise Renewal Call

What.Name: Arlo Technologies, Inc - Khoros - Renewal - 1702564 - 4_2026_ Community & Care

Description:
MEETING SUMMARY
* Meeting date and time: 04/09/2026
* Meeting attendees:
* Sebastian Desand (Trilogy, Renewals)
* Ledia Dilo (Arlo, Global Customer Experience & Operations Executive)
* James Cross (Arlo, community/platform lead)
* Meeting length: ~26 minutes
Talking points:
Contract redlines and legal process
* Customer initiated call to review contract redlines live to avoid ongoing back-and-forth with legal teams.
* Sebastian explained internal legal process:
* Certain clauses are non-negotiable (“red flags”)
* Others require escalation to leadership
* Some can be handled directly by legal
* Customer emphasized they want to accelerate decision-making and avoid delays.
Customer escalation authority
* Lydia stated she is final decision-maker on their side:
* “I am as high as it gets”
* Can negotiate terms directly without further internal delay
* Requested equivalent decision-making authority from vendor side.
Renewal risk clearly stated
* Customer explicitly warned:
* “The renewal is completely at risk here at this point”
* Frustration with prolonged legal negotiations and inefficiency.
Payment terms discussion
* Standard customer terms: 45 days
* Willing to concede to 30 days if required
* Not considered a major blocker.
Auto-renewal and termination clauses
* Strong objection to auto-renewal clauses:
* Customer wants flexibility to renegotiate at renewal
* Position:
* Accept termination for cause (non-negotiable)
* Termination for convenience not required
* Likely willingness to remove auto-renewal clause
* Highlighted imbalance of auto-renewal without termination flexibility.
Pricing and price protection concerns
* Reiterated dissatisfaction with 25% price increase:
* “I've never been hit with a 25% increase”
* “We got blindsided”
* Key issue: no price protection for future renewals
* Customer requested clarity on future price caps (e.g., 2–5% COLA)
* Concern over compounding increases (25% + 25%).
Vendor pricing strategy
* Sebastian confirmed strategy:
* Price increases on short-term contracts
* Price lock incentives for long-term (e.g., 5-year no increase)
* Customer open to long-term in future, but not before validating platform and relationship.
Relationship concerns
* Customer explicitly stated:
* “This doesn’t feel like a partnership”
* “We’ve got to build some trust”
* Perception that vendor is unwilling to compromise on legal terms despite price increase already accepted.
Customer concessions
* Customer confirmed willingness to:
* Accept 25% price increase (reluctantly)
* Be flexible on payment terms
* Expectation: vendor should reciprocate on legal terms.
Strategic renewal framing
* Customer made clear this renewal is critical for future relationship:
* “This discussion… is really for next year”
* Outcome will determine whether vendor retains business long-term
* Indicated RFP likely if experience does not improve.
Internal pressure and inefficiency
* Customer noted high internal effort:
* “I’m over indexing on time here”
* Legal team considers many vendor changes unreasonable vs industry standard.
Next steps and escalation
* Sebastian to:
* Escalate internally (VP, CFO) for approvals on key clauses
* Push for faster turnaround due to timeline
* Plan to revert with updated contract version by Monday/Tuesday
* Follow-up checkpoint call scheduled for Tuesday

INSIGHTS
* Renewal likelihood: Short-term likely, long-term at risk
* Customer confirmed willingness to proceed with renewal despite concerns.
* However, long-term retention is highly uncertain.
* Key renewal driver: Legal flexibility and partnership perception
* Customer has already conceded on price.
* Now expects reciprocity via legal concessions.
* Major risk factors:
* Damaged trust at start of IgniteTech relationship
* Perception of inflexibility (“unwillingness to cooperate”)
* Lack of price protection for future years
* High internal friction and inefficiency
* Explicit churn signal:
* “This doesn’t feel like a partnership”
* “The ball is in your court”
* “This will determine whether you have our business in the future”
* Policy conflicts (likely non-compliant):
* Requests to remove auto-renewal clauses
* Requests for price caps / future price protections
* Flexibility on standard legal positions
* Opportunity:
* Customer is still committed to renewing now
* Strong executive engagement → chance to repair relationship if handled correctly

ACTIONS FROM THE MEETING
* Sebastian (Khoros):
* Escalate contract terms internally (VP, CFO, legal) – immediate
* Seek approvals on:
* Payment terms flexibility
* Auto-renewal removal
* Other redlined clauses
* Return updated contract version by Monday/Tuesday
* Customer (Lydia / James):
* Remain available for rapid decision-making
* Attend follow-up checkpoint call (Tuesday)
* Joint:
* Schedule and attend follow-up call Tuesday (early PST)

Subject: Enterprise Renewal Call
Description:
MEETING SUMMARY
* Meeting date: 04/08/2026
* Meeting attendees:
* Sebastian Desand (Trilogy, Renewals)
* Mandy Ross (Databricks, Community owner)
* Amber Samlich (Databricks, Procurement)
* Meeting length: ~21 minutes

Talking points:
Pricing increase objection
* Databricks raised strong concern over a 25% price increase with no scope change.
* Customer stated this is “very large for a year every year” and outside acceptable thresholds.
* Procurement clarified acceptable increases are ~3% or CPI (whichever is lower).
* Customer explicitly stated: “we don’t even have approval at this price point”.
Multi-year vs one-year renewal
* Khoros proposed multi-year (3-year) to offset increase via price lock and added value.
* Databricks confirmed multi-year is not an option due to internal policy across vendors.
Rebundling and added features
* Customer pushed back on rebundling strategy:
* “we didn’t ask for additional features”
* Seen as industry-wide tactic to justify price increases
* Additional features not considered valuable enough to justify cost increase.
Aurora migration and value discussion
* Vendor positioned free migration to Khoros Aurora (previously $50K–$125K) as added value.
* Customer rejected this as justification:
* “this isn’t really something that we need”
* Migration perceived as vendor-driven, not customer-driven.
Vendor position on pricing
* Sebastian confirmed 25% increase is a global leadership mandate with no exceptions.
* Stated multiple escalations have been denied regardless of customer size or tenure.
Escalation request
* Customer requested escalation to someone with pricing authority.
* Vendor agreed to arrange call with VP but set expectation no exceptions likely.
Churn risk / competitive pressure
* Procurement clearly stated:
* “our recommendation… is to go to market and to evaluate others” if pricing not resolved.
* Mandy outlined fallback plan:
* Stay on Khoros Classic
* Run RFP
* Move to another vendor
* Direct quote: “this is ridiculous” (re pricing).
Product and relationship concerns
* Customer indicated relationship issues with IgniteTech.
* Aurora seen as “unproven in our environment”.
* UAT has not yet started, increasing risk of migration.
Timeline pressure
* Renewal deadline in ~2 weeks, creating risk of service interruption.
* Internal approvals (procurement, finance, legal) still pending.
Legal / MSA discussion
* Customer asked to reuse existing MSA to save time.
* Vendor to confirm if legacy agreement is still valid post-acquisition.
* New MSA would significantly delay process.
Customer leverage and strategic value
* Mandy highlighted Databricks’ technical sophistication:
* “we are Databricks… already using your REST APIs to build apps”
* Implicit signal of strategic customer value.

INSIGHTS
* Renewal likelihood: At risk / low confidence
* Strong resistance to pricing with clear internal thresholds (3% vs 25%).
* Explicit procurement stance to initiate RFP if no movement.
* Customer has viable fallback (stay on Classic short-term + switch vendor).
* Key renewal driver: Pricing flexibility
* Without reduction, deal is unlikely to close.
* Customer has clearly stated current pricing will not be approved.
* Secondary risks:
* Weak vendor relationship (specific mention of IgniteTech concerns)
* Lack of confidence in Aurora (unproven, forced migration perception)
* Timing pressure (legal + approvals vs 2-week deadline)
* Policy conflicts (likely non-compliant requests):
* Request for price reduction below mandated 25% increase
* Expectation of minimal increase (~3%)
* One-year renewal only (limits vendor leverage via multi-year)
* Vendor constraint:
* Pricing is centrally mandated with no exceptions historically approved, creating a major deal blocker.

ACTIONS FROM THE MEETING
* Sebastian (Khoros):
* Schedule escalation call with VP – target: Monday
* Brief VP on customer position
* Confirm whether existing MSA can be reused – ASAP follow-up via email
* Databricks (Procurement & Mandy):
* Attend escalation call with VP (Monday)
* Continue internal discussions on pricing approval
* Mandy to brief her manager on situation (same day)
* Internal (Khoros):
* Prepare escalation strategy despite pricing constraints
* Assess legal feasibility of using existing MSA

Subject: Check-in Call
Description:
Call on MSTeams, no recording allowed. Manual transcript.

Meeting Summary

* Meeting date and time: Not specified (transcript starts mid-call)
* Meeting attendees: Tim Courtenay (Supplier - Khoros/IgniteTech), Caroline Braswell (Truist - Social Media/Vendor Management), Alan Anseeuw (Truist - Senior Category Manager, Procurement)

Talking points

1. Roles and responsibilities

* Caroline manages vendor relations, governance, and day-to-day ownership of Khoros from a social media and operational perspective. She is a former daily user and still considers herself a “power user.”
* Alan manages procurement and contracting, including renewals, legal coordination, and sourcing decisions.

2. Long-term relationship with Khoros

* Truist has used Khoros (formerly Spredfast) for **8–9 years**, dating back to SunTrust.
* Caroline: “This has been our tool for eight-nine years… we’ve been very close with this product.”
* Strong historical reliance and familiarity with the platform.

3. Product value and strengths

* Key strengths highlighted:

* Ease of use
* Separation of marketing and care workflows
* Strong reporting and agent experience
* Critical **data retention and audit capability** for regulatory compliance
* Caroline: archive capability is “a huge driver of this relationship” and supports regulatory requirements.

4. Impact of IgniteTech acquisition

* Truist was **not informed prior to acquisition**, which caused concern.
* Caroline: “That was a shock to us and something that we were not expecting.”
* Post-acquisition changes have negatively impacted confidence.

5. Major pain point – loss of X (Twitter) integration

* Identified as the **biggest issue** since acquisition.
* Caroline: “That has been a pain point… X is a big platform for us… that is probably our biggest pain point… to be totally transparent.”
* This has reduced current value and disrupted BAU operations.

6. Product roadmap and recovery

* Tim communicated that:

* X integration is expected to be restored in the new unified platform
* Care + Marketing will merge into a single platform (formerly “Khoros One”)
* Additional capabilities (e.g., community module) will be bundled
* Customer interested but cautious; needs clarity and proof.

7. Pricing and commercial concerns

* Tim flagged **likely price increase** due to IgniteTech model.
* Strong pushback from Alan:

* “Price increase automatically concerns me.”
* “Trying to understand why there’s a price increase if you’re covering less.”
* Customer requires **clear justification and value mapping**.

8. Contracting and procurement complexity

* Truist requires:

* Preference for **their own paper (MSA)** over supplier paper
* Extensive legal, risk, and compliance review
* Completion of all due diligence items before execution
* Existing contract has **~13 amendments**, creating complexity.
* Internal process includes legal, sourcing, risk, and business approvals.

9. Due diligence and risk requirements

* Outstanding “sub-issues” from risk assessments (via Archer Engage) must be resolved.
* Mostly documentation requests (e.g., third-party risk management, SOC 2).
* These are **mandatory blockers** to contract execution.

10. Renewal timeline

* Renewal date: **June 20** (~2 months from call)
* Timeline risk depends on:

* Contract negotiations
* Risk/compliance completion
* Internal approvals
* Alan: timeline cannot be confirmed until draft is reviewed.

11. Customer sentiment and renewal intent

* Customer prefers to **maintain continuity** rather than switch vendors.
* Caroline: “We’re not trying to uproot and change a bunch of things.”
* However:

* “This has not been great.”
* Priority is to **restore confidence and functionality (especially X)** before committing.

12. Future usage and expansion

* Short-term: return to **status quo**
* Longer-term: possible interest in community module, but **not a current priority**

Insights

* Renewal likelihood is **conditional** on resolving key issues, especially X integration and pricing justification.
* Strong historical loyalty is a positive indicator, but **recent disruption has weakened trust**.
* The **loss of X integration + proposed price increase** is a high-risk combination.
* Procurement environment is highly complex and **process-heavy**, increasing timeline risk.
* Truist is unlikely to accept:

* Multi-year deals (due to funding model)
* Supplier paper over their own MSA
* Price increases without clear value justification
* Customer sentiment: cautiously willing to renew but requires confidence restoration.

Actions from the Meeting

* Tim Courtenay:

* Send full renewal package (Word versions + pricing + product mapping + justification) by Friday
* Provide explanation for price increase and value changes
* Review and respond to Archer Engage due diligence items
* Align internally with Abigail on X roadmap and risk items
* Caroline Braswell:

* Review order form and product alignment
* Share/confirm Archer Engage issues and ensure correct routing
* Schedule follow-up with Abigail for roadmap discussion
* Alan Anseeuw:

* Coordinate legal review and contracting process
* Provide feedback after reviewing draft documents
* Truist Legal Team:

* Review MSA/amendments and provide feedback (expected within 1–2 weeks)

Summary for Salesforce Opportunity Description field
Check-in call with Caroline Braswell (Social Media/Vendor Management) and Alan Anseeuw (Procurement) at Truist regarding upcoming Khoros renewal. Discussion covered product usage, strong historical relationship (8–9 years), key strengths (ease of use, reporting, compliance/archiving), and major concerns following IgniteTech acquisition—particularly loss of X integration and potential price increase. Customer requires full visibility into product changes, pricing justification, and resolution of compliance items before progressing. Renewal sentiment is cautiously positive but dependent on restoring confidence and functionality.

Actions:

* Supplier to send full renewal package (Word docs, pricing, product mapping, justification) by Friday
* Supplier to address X integration roadmap and due diligence items
* Customer to review documents and provide feedback
* Customer to progress legal, procurement, and risk reviews
* Follow-up meeting to be scheduled after review

Sales next step summary
Send complete renewal package including Word documents, pricing justification, and product mapping, and begin resolving due diligence items to unblock procurement process. Required by Friday. 04/11/2026 Tim Courtenay

Probable Outcome: Undetermined
Probable Outcome Notes: Net risk is medium-high. Customer has strong historical loyalty and preference to renew, but significant risks remain due to loss of X integration, reduced confidence post-acquisition, strict procurement requirements, and resistance to price increases. Renewal depends on restoring functionality, resolving compliance items, and clearly justifying pricing.

Follow-Up Email

Hi Caroline, hi Alan,

Thank you for your time today. It was helpful to understand your current usage of Khoros and the key considerations ahead of the renewal.

Here’s a summary of what we discussed:

* Truist has been using Khoros (formerly Spredfast) for 8–9 years, with strong reliance on the platform across both marketing and care use cases
* Key strengths of the platform include ease of use, separation of workflows, reporting capabilities, and the ability to retain and retrieve historical social interactions for compliance purposes
* The loss of X (Twitter) integration has been a significant pain point and has impacted your day-to-day operations
* You would like clarity on how the product is evolving post-acquisition, including packaging, feature changes, and how capabilities map from your current setup
* Pricing will be an important area of focus, particularly given the current gap in functionality, and will require clear justification
* Your internal process includes legal, procurement, and risk reviews, and all due diligence items (via Archer Engage) must be resolved before execution
* You are working through existing agreements (including amendments) to determine the best path forward (amendment vs new MSA)
* Overall, the goal is to return to a stable “status quo” and rebuild confidence before considering any future expansion

Next steps:

* Tim (Supplier):

* Send full renewal package (Word versions of order form/SOW, product mapping, and pricing justification) by Friday
* Provide additional context on pricing changes and product evolution
* Review and respond to outstanding due diligence items

* Caroline (Truist):

* Review documentation and confirm product alignment
* Follow up on Archer Engage items and coordinate with Abigail on roadmap discussion

* Alan (Truist):

* Review contractual documents and coordinate legal/procurement feedback

Once you’ve had a chance to review the materials, we can schedule a follow-up session to go through any questions and next steps.

Please let me know if I’ve missed anything or if you’d like to add further points.

Best regards,
Tim

Subject: Renewal Call
Description:
read.ai | ServiceTitan, Inc - Khoros Renewal (Meet Meeting) | Wed. Apr 8, 2026

Full Notes and Transcript: https://app.read.ai/analytics/meetings/01KNPSEBJSPSY7N6D9D0ZRV9Q8

Meeting ID: 01KNPSEBJSPSY7N6D9D0ZRV9Q8

3 Participants | James Quigley, Kristi Harrington, Lindsay Gray

----

Summary:

The meeting reviewed ServiceTitan’s upcoming Khoros renewal, historical pricing, the renewal offer, and decision next steps. Attendees confirmed last year’s contract price was $72,500 and that the vendor’s proposed 12‑month renewal price is $91,875. James offered to email a detailed price comparison and the prior contract to Kristi so she can present options to her leaders. The group agreed Kristi will use that material to brief leadership and weigh the renewal options.

Participants also discussed account management and outreach gaps, with Kristi noting limited contact from an account manager and requesting an introduction to the new representative. James agreed to introduce the new rep and to send information on a three‑year renewal option and on the "prime" add‑on products, Kayako and Contently. Kristi indicated the add‑ons are unlikely to be adopted now, leaving adoption decisions for later evaluation by her team.

----

Chapters & Topics:

Introductions and recording notice
Renewal context and historical pricing
-Kristi needs clarity on renewal timing and costs to brief her leadership and plan next steps.
-The prior contract price was $72,500 and the proposed 12‑month renewal price is $91,875.
Support ticket and account manager contact issues
-Kristi reported minimal account manager contact and requested an introduction to the new rep.
Prime program and add‑on products discussion
Request for account manager introduction
Renewal pricing, options, and deadlines
-James will provide a comparison between one‑year and three‑year options and include the prior contract for Kristi's review.
-The current contract contains a 60‑day renewal clause with a renewal date of July 10.
Follow‑up commitments and closing

----

Action Items:

-James Quigley will introduce the new account manager to Kristi Harrington and Lindsay Gray
-James Quigley will email Kristi Harrington the renewal pricing details, the one‑year vs three‑year comparison, the prior contract copy, and information about the prime program products

----

Key Questions:

-Will a new account representative contact us?
-What will the renewal price be and is there a percentage increase compared to last year?
-Do you need to decide on renewal by September?

Subject: Enterprise Renewal Call

What.Name: AS Helmes - Instru optiikka Oy - Aurea Platform - Renewal - 1727628 - 7_2026

Description:
read.ai | Aurea Platform Renewal Call (Meet Meeting) | Thu. Feb 5, 2026

Full Notes and Transcript: https://app.read.ai/analytics/meetings/01KGPXTA2BHGF8CVP55CVQRVA5

Meeting ID: 01KGPXTA2BHGF8CVP55CVQRVA5

2 Participants | James Stothard, Kaupo Grünberg

----

Summary:

The meeting focused on an unexpected requirement to renew a long-standing reseller agreement with Aurea for the Messenger product and the customer- and legal-related implications of that renewal. Participants reviewed that a historic agreement dating from around 2013 had not been flagged as non‑automatically renewing until raised by Tim, and James indicated he expects only minor modernizing updates, has requested inclusion on the legal ticket, and will prepare and circulate an updated reseller agreement for review. Kaupo noted the historic document contains obsolete sections and may require structural updates.

Discussion covered customer impact from Aurea’s floor-price policy, which has pushed several smaller customers toward migration plans while larger, heavily integrated accounts remain stable. Near-term renewals of concern include Axfood and Instru/Instra, with SEB Bank and a Swedish municipality cited as stable accounts and the Central Bank of Estonia identified as at risk. The group also evaluated Messenger’s position: it is an older product not targeted for new sales but still valuable for large, integration‑heavy customers (British Airways cited), and recent development work has begun to address earlier degradation on legacy Java releases that previously constrained renewals.

----

Chapters & Topics:

Audio and introductions
-The call began with audio issues that were resolved early in the meeting.
Meeting platform preferences and logistics
Renewal process familiarity and reseller involvement
Surprise reseller-agreement renewal and history
-Kaupo and James agreed the reseller agreement renewal surfaced unexpectedly and likely only needs modernization.
-The existing reseller agreement appears to date from around 2013 and has been used continuously since Sonic was acquired by Aurea.
-Aurea’s floor-price implementation has driven several customers to plan migrations away from Aurea Messenger.
Customer impact of Aurea floor price and specific accounts (Axfood, Instru)
-Axfood and Instru/Instra are the two near-term renewals and are the primary migration risks discussed.
-Kaupo will attempt to reactivate and assess Axfood and Instru accounts in February to determine migration status.
Remaining portfolio stability and risk assessment
-SEB Bank and a Swedish municipality renewed recently and are considered more stable accounts.
Agreement updates, roles, and next steps
-James has requested inclusion on the legal ticket to review the new reseller agreement and expects minimal changes.
Integration positioning and sales implications
-Modern modernization projects typically include integration as part of the project rather than purchasing a separate integration layer
-Aurea Messenger is an older product that still works for many customers but is not positioned as a new-sale product
Product status and customer retention
-Large customers with extensive integrations (e.g., British Airways) remain on Messenger and retain value from its capabilities
Portfolio coverage and business-unit structure
Pricing, support burden, and product maintenance
-Messenger was moved to minimum pricing/floor price, which impacted smaller accounts more severely than larger agreements
-Product updates and more active development have occurred recently, which may improve customer experience and renewal discussions
Reseller agreement and renewal planning
-James will prepare and send a reseller agreement and will consult legal about necessary updates
-Kaupo expects the reseller agreement will require structural updates because older sections (rebates, pricing) are obsolete
Team coordination and meeting close

----

Action Items:

-Kaupo will investigate and try to determine the rationale behind Instru’s one‑year renewal decision and report back
-Kaupo will activate both Axfood and Instru accounts and assess their migration status during February
-James will be added to the legal ticket for the reseller agreement and follow the ticket updates
-James will share draft reseller/distributor agreement updates with Kaupo when available
-James will assemble and send the reseller agreement to Kaupo as soon as possible
-James will check with the legal team about required updates to the reseller agreement
-James will send renewal details for the July renewals to Kaupo (if needed)
-Kaupo will get feedback from the Axfood/customer team regarding the renewal quotations before proceeding

----

Key Questions:

-How long will the customers’ migrations away from Aurea take and will they succeed?
-Are you handling only AutoMessenger renewals or the whole product-portfolio renewals?
-Do the customers have sufficient lead time before their Renewal dates?

Subject: Check-in Call

What.Name: Scottish & Southern Electricity Networks - Khoros - Renewal - 1707763 - 7_2026

Description:
Call was in customer Teams account, no recording attendant allowed. Manual transcription.

Meeting Summary

Meeting date and time: April 2, 2026
Meeting attendees: Tim Courtenay (VP of Sales, Trilogy/IgniteTech), Kirsty Mackle (Junior Contracts Manager, SSEN).

Talking points:

Introduction and Roles: Tim explained the relationship between Trilogy, IgniteTech, and Khoros. Kirsty confirmed her role as a Junior Contracts Manager in procurement, focusing on the renewal itself, while Bradley is the primary user and business unit holder.

Status of Renewal: Kirsty stated that the Khoros renewal is currently progressing through internal discussions and is with the SSEN legal team. She noted, "it is going ahead, it is progressing."

Compliance and Documentation: Tim referenced documentation sent by Asad on March 23rd, including the DTIA and business continuity excerpt. Kirsty will verify with legal (Sophie Graham and Mark Williams) if anything further is required.

Contract Notice Period: Tim reminded Kirsty of the 90-day notice period in the current contract.

Pricing and Quotes: Tim noted that a formal renewal quote hasn't been sent yet. Kirsty will check if Bradley received an early offer, but Tim will provide a fresh quote following the call.

Entitlement Changes: Tim highlighted changes in the Khoros packaging. The renewal includes a move to "unlimited seats" and a shift from the old "Premium" support plan to the new "Success" plans (Standard, Gold, Platinum).

Support Requirements: A key decision point is whether SSEN requires 24/7 support (Platinum Success). Kirsty noted that Bradley would need to confirm this requirement.

AI Features: Tim introduced the new generative AI add-on features. Kirsty mentioned that their current "go-to" stance is to keep things similar to the current setup and not switch on AI unless needed, stating, "I think we're just wanting to keep it as similar to what we have currently."

Administrative Updates: The contracting entity address for Khoros LLC has changed to the corporate headquarters in Austin, Texas. Kirsty will check if this needs updating in their systems.

Timeline: Tim proposed finalizing commercials in early May and closing all paperwork by late June for the July 30th renewal date.

Renewal Term: Kirsty indicated they are likely looking at a one-year renewal. Tim informed her that a one-year renewal would see a price increase, whereas a three-year renewal could flatten the price and remove usage-based limits.

Insights:

Likelihood of Renewing: High. Kirsty explicitly stated that the renewal is "going ahead" and is currently with legal for terms and conditions review.

Renewal Drivers: The renewal seems driven by the critical nature of the use case (customer care during emergencies/storms). The final package (support level and AI) will be determined by Bradley.

Policy Constraints: The customer's preference for a 1-year renewal conflicts with our desire for longer-term stability; Tim has already "sensitized" her to the fact that a 1-year term will result in a price increase.

Use Case Insight: The platform is a critical customer interface tool used during emergency power situations and storm aftermaths.

Actions from the Meeting:

Provide a renewal quote (one-year and three-year options) to Kirsty and Bradley - Tim Courtenay - ASAP.

Send follow-up notes/record of the meeting - Tim Courtenay - by April 4th/5th.

Check with legal (Sophie and Mark) regarding outstanding compliance docs - Kirsty Mackle - April 8th.

Verify if an early renewal quote was already received - Kirsty Mackle - April 8th.

Check and update the Khoros LLC contracting address in the SSEN system - Kirsty Mackle - April 8th.

Review the new packaging/support levels and AI interest with Bradley - Tim/Kirsty/Bradley - Week of April 13th.

Schedule follow-up call - Tim Courtenay - Target week of April 13th.

Summary for the Salesforce Opportunity Description field
Renewal call between Tim Courtenay (Trilogy) and Kirsty Mackle (SSEN procurement) to discuss the Khoros renewal due July 30th. SSEN confirmed the renewal is progressing through legal. Discussion covered updated packaging (unlimited seats), the shift to "Success" support plans, and the introduction of AI features. Tim highlighted that a 1-year renewal will involve a price increase, while a 3-year term offers price protection and removed usage limits.

Actions:

Provide renewal quotes (1yr vs 3yr) - Tim Courtenay.

Send meeting notes - Tim Courtenay.

Check compliance docs status with legal - Kirsty Mackle.

Confirm received quotes and update vendor address - Kirsty Mackle.

Review support levels and AI with Bradley - Tim/Kirsty/Bradley.

Schedule next call for week of April 13th - Tim Courtenay.

Sales next step summary
Send 1-year and 3-year renewal quotes and schedule a follow-up call with Bradley and Kirsty to finalize the support level and commercial terms. 04/02/2024 Tim Courtenay.

Probable Outcome

Probable Outcome: Likely to Win

Probable Outcome Notes: Net risk is low. The customer confirmed the renewal is already in the legal review phase and described Khoros as a critical tool for emergency customer support. The main variables are the final support tier and the term length.

Follow-up email:

Subject: Follow-up: SSEN / Khoros Renewal Discussion

Hi Kirsty,

It was a pleasure meeting you today to discuss the upcoming Khoros renewal for SSEN.

Following our conversation, I have summarized the key points we discussed:

Renewal Status: You confirmed that the renewal process is underway internally and is currently being reviewed by your legal team.

Compliance & Documentation: We noted the DTIA and business continuity documentation sent by Asad on March 23rd. You will be checking with Sophie Graham and Mark Williams to see if any further information is required from our side.

Contracting Entity: We discussed the address change for Khoros LLC. Please let me know if you require any specific documentation to update this in your vendor system.

Updated Packaging: I highlighted that the new Khoros packaging now includes unlimited seats. We also touched on the transition from "Premium Support" to our new "Success" tiers (Standard, Gold, and Platinum).

AI Features: We introduced the new generative AI capabilities. While SSEN’s current stance is to keep the setup consistent with the current implementation, these features remain available as an opt-in add-on.

Renewal Terms: We discussed the July 30th renewal date and the 90-day notice period. I noted that while a one-year renewal will see a price increase, a three-year commitment would allow us to flatten that pricing and remove usage-based limits (seats, visits, API calls).

Next Steps & Actions:

Tim Courtenay: I will send over a formal renewal quote providing both one-year and three-year options for your review.

Kirsty Mackle: You will check with your legal team regarding compliance docs and verify the current vendor address on your system.

Tim/Kirsty/Bradley: We will aim to meet during the week of April 13th to review the specific requirements for support levels and discuss Bradley's feedback on the new entitlements.

Please let me know if there is anything else you would like to add to these notes. I look forward to our next discussion.

Best regards,

Tim Courtenay
VP of Sales, Trilogy

Subject: Check-in Call
Description:
Meeting Summary
* Meeting date and time: 04/01/2026
* Meeting attendees:
* Sebastian Desand (Trilogy Renewals / Khoros)
* Eric Sharrow (H&R Block – Procurement)
* Meeting length: ~36 minutes
Talking points:
1. Purpose of call:
* Early-stage renewal discussion for Khoros Care ahead of July 31st renewal date.
* Focus on understanding current usage, renewal intent, and introducing new offerings.
2. Customer current position on renewal:
* Likely to renew but not fully committed.
* Customer stated: “I would probably… like 80%… maybe even like 90.”
* Decision will depend heavily on pricing and internal alignment.
* Customer emphasized transparency: will not push for concessions unless confident deal will close.
3. Current contract and usage:
* Approx. $100K annual contract value
* 20 seats currently in use
* No immediate need for additional licenses
* Usage largely unchanged from previous year
4. Customer feedback on product:
* Positive on usability:
* “They truly enjoy the interface… it makes sense.”
* Historical concern:
* Poor support responsiveness and account turnover (during prior RFP phase)
* No recent complaints, but status needs validation post-tax season.
5. Internal customer situation:
* Organizational changes:
* Team restructuring (downsizing and role changes)
* New reporting line under VP of IT (Paul Clark)
* Key stakeholder: Patrick Pearl (Director)
* Decision-making still stabilizing.
6. Timing constraints:
* Business heavily impacted by tax season (until April 15th)
* Meaningful engagement expected late April / early May
* Renewal deadline: July 31st
7. Pricing sensitivity:
* Customer explicitly concerned about potential increase:
* If pricing increases significantly, may revisit RFP / alternative vendors
* Sebastian confirmed pricing will remain “in the ballpark” (not a major increase).
8. New commercial model introduced:
* Shift to bundle/enterprise model:
* Unlimited seats
* Additional products included (Khoros One, Community)
* Pricing not dependent on usage of entitlements.
9. Khoros One discussion:
* Customer not familiar with offering
* Strong interest in learning more, especially:
* Marketing use cases
* Cross-team value (beyond current Care use case)
* Customer sees opportunity to expand usage across business units.
10. Demo and enablement:
* Customer requested materials (links, docs) to share internally
* Goal: build interest before scheduling demo
* Potential to include marketing team stakeholders in evaluation.
1. Contracting discussion:
* Supplier wants to move customer to standardized contract platform
* Customer prefers to stay on existing terms if possible (to avoid delays)
* Legal review required on both sides.

Insights:
* Renewal likelihood is high but not guaranteed:
* Strong baseline dependency on product
* Risk tied primarily to pricing increases
* Customer is procurement-led and cost-conscious:
* Will re-open competitive process if pricing is unfavorable
* Expansion opportunity:
* Khoros One + enterprise model could unlock cross-functional adoption (marketing team)
* Timing risk:
* Limited engagement before mid-April due to tax season
* Contracting risk:
* Moving to new legal terms may slow or complicate renewal
* Key takeaway:
* This is a defensive renewal with upsell potential, but success depends on:
* Controlled pricing
* Effective internal enablement (materials + demo)

Actions from the Meeting
* Sebastian Desand:
* Send Khoros One materials / links to Eric – ASAP
* Prepare and send renewal presentation – ASAP
* Provide contract/T&Cs (pending legal review) – TBD
* Review existing contract + amendment with legal – ASAP
* Eric Sharrow:
* Share materials with Patrick and internal stakeholders – ASAP
* Identify marketing stakeholders for potential demo – TBD
* Provide post-tax season feedback on usage and support experience – late April
* Both:
* Schedule demo (likely late April / early May)
* Continue renewal discussions with pricing – TBD

Subject: Enterprise Renewal Call
Description:
Meeting Summary
* Meeting date: 03/30/2026
* Meeting attendees:
* Sebastian Desand (Trilogy Renewals)
* Robert Werner (Procurement – Global IT Software & Services)
* Anton Hallinger (IT/Technical)
* Anusha Govindu (IT-Lead for community platform)
* Meeting length: ~29 minutes
Talking points:
1. Introduction of stakeholder structure on customer side:
* Procurement, IT, and Contract/Vendor Management operate jointly in renewal decisions.
2. Supplier presented renewal options for Khoros Community:
* Current contract: 36 months including:
* 20M monthly API calls
* 8.75M annual visits
* 190 success services
* Proposed 25% price increase across all customers, mandated by leadership due to unsustainable pricing model.
3. Justification for price increase:
* Khoros financial model deemed unsustainable.
* Investment required for future development (primarily Aurora platform).
* Increase applied globally, not customer-specific.
4. Renewal options presented:
* 1-year option:
* Includes free Aurora upgrade (valued ~100K)
* Increased API and visit limits
* 3-year option (Ultimate tier):
* Unlimited API calls, visits, and AI usage
* Price lock for 3 years
* Includes Aurora upgrade and AI capabilities
5. Discussion on Khoros One (care and marketing platform):
* Customer previously used it but stated:
* “It did not pay anything for us back.”
* Open to re-evaluating if improvements demonstrated.
* Interest in demo, especially regarding AI capabilities and integration with Aurora.
6. Clarification on Aurora platform:
* Successor to Khoros Classic.
* Khoros Classic confirmed as:
* “feature complete”
* No further development, only maintenance and bug fixes.
7. Customer position on strategy:
* Will remain on Khoros Classic for at least 1 more year.
* Migration decision (Aurora or alternative vendor) expected after internal planning cycle (post-May, new fiscal year from September).
* Migration not feasible in short term due to budget and planning constraints.
8. Customer pushback on pricing:
* Strong objection to price increase for a declining product:
* “There needs to be a price decline… [as] there’s no investment anymore happening.”
* Requested pricing to reflect reduced product value.
* Position: only willing to consider 1-year renewal at reduced cost.
9. Supplier response to pricing objection:
* Confirmed no flexibility on 25% increase, as it is a global mandate.
* No ability to discount or adjust pricing, even for unchanged scope.
10. Contractual discussion:
* Customer questioned whether prior contract limited renewal increases.
* Supplier clarified limits applied only within current term, not to new contract.
Timeline:
* Renewal date: August 5th
* Customer expects to have clearer strategic direction by May.

Insights:
* Customer is not currently considering churn in the immediate term, but is actively evaluating future platform options.
* Short-term intent: 1-year renewal only, purely to maintain operations.
* Strong resistance to pricing model due to:
* Product being in maintenance mode (“declining product”)
* Lack of ongoing investment in Khoros Classic
* Customer explicitly expects price reduction, which we cannot accommodate.
* This creates significant commercial tension and renewal risk beyond 1 year.
* Customer is open to Aurora and Khoros One, but only after internal strategic alignment.
* The Aurora migration incentive (free upgrade) may lose impact if not aligned with customer timing.
* Renewal likelihood:
* Short-term (1-year): moderate to high (operational dependency)
* Long-term: at risk due to active market evaluation

Actions from the Meeting
* Sebastian Desand: Send renewal presentation to customer – ASAP
* Sebastian Desand: Arrange follow-up call after customer internal review – TBD
* Customer (Procurement/IT team): Review renewal options and pricing internally – before next discussion
* Customer: Define strategic direction (Aurora vs alternatives) – expected by May

Subject: Cancellation Call

What.Name: Virginia Rural Water Association (VRWA) - GoMembers - Renewal - 7_2026

Description:
Meeting Summary
Meeting date and time: Not specified
Meeting attendees: James Stothard (Supplier), Lisa Kerr (Customer – Virginia Rural Water)
Meeting length: ~11 minutes

Talking points:

Renewal discussion for GoMembers ahead of contract end date (1st July) and termination deadline (1st May).
Customer had previously submitted a cancellation request but withdrew it due to misunderstanding that cancellation would be immediate. Clarified that cancellation stops renewal/sales activity but access continues until contract end (1st July).
Customer confirmed they have not reviewed new pricing and only received a standard renewal notice without pricing details.
Customer confirmed decision to move away from GoMembers is effectively final: “Yes.”
Reason for churn is not product functionality, but:
“continual price increases and lack of support”
“anything that has to be done now… Rebecca just has to figure it out on her own”
No meaningful support or account management “for a couple of years”
Sales rep explained new pricing structure:
New minimum price: $25,600/year
Customer currently paying ~$5,000/year
Increase driven by unsustainable financial model and need to fund support and development
Customer acknowledged pricing rationale but confirmed decision to leave remains unchanged.
Customer has been preparing migration for 1.5–2 years, led by Rebecca:
Working with external provider
Gradual transition to avoid risk
Testing components and redesigning website already underway
Migration is in progress but not fully complete; some risk of “hiccups” acknowledged.
Sales rep highlighted no short-term extensions available (minimum 1-year renewal), advising customer to complete migration before July.
Customer confirmed internal awareness and board alignment on moving platforms and associated investment.
Agreement that customer will resend cancellation notice; sales rep will facilitate and confirm continued access until July 1st.

Insights:

Customer is highly unlikely to renew; decision has been in progress for up to 2 years and is already operationalized.
Primary churn drivers:
Lack of support and customer success engagement over multiple years
Price increases without perceived value increase
Customer explicitly stated: “the support… definitely has been lacking” and linked this directly to their decision.
Migration is already underway and near completion, reducing likelihood of reversal.
New pricing ($25.6k vs $5k) would represent a >5x increase, further reinforcing churn.
No indication that improved support or commercial concessions would recover the account at this stage.
Customer expectations around support would likely not be fully recoverable in short term even if addressed now.
No unrealistic requests made (e.g., discounts, short-term renewal), but customer would not accept standard pricing or terms regardless.
Actions from the Meeting
Customer (Lisa Kerr / Rebecca):
Resend cancellation notice – ASAP (before May 1st deadline)
Continue migration and testing of new platform – before July 1st
Supplier (James Stothard):
Offer to forward cancellation internally and ensure processing – upon receipt
Send email confirmation that access will remain until July 1st after cancellation submission

Subject: Check-in Call

What.Name: Egon Zehnder International AG - Messageone - Renewal - 8_2026

Description:
Call Summary – Egon, Sender International AG (EMS / MessageOne)

Attendees:

Egon (Sender International AG)
Desh Deepak
James Stothard
Situation Overview

Customer is high-risk (active churn consideration) driven by a prolonged breakdown in support and account engagement.

Key Issues
1. Support Failure
Multiple open technical cases with no resolution
No responses from support or prior contacts (Joe, Kathy, Tony, Omar)
Escalations—including to CEO level—received no response
Customer unable to progress or escalate issues
2. Account Management Gap
Customer unaware of current AM (Jamie Alvarez)
No proactive outreach from Aurea/Trilogy side
Perception of abandonment
3. Product Confidence Risk
Customer believed MessageOne may no longer be supported
Triggered market evaluation of alternatives
Renewal Context
Renewal Date: August 20
Termination Deadline: June 21
Budget Status: Already allocated for this cycle
Customer Position:
Willing to renew if confidence is restored
Otherwise prepared to switch vendors
Open Questions / Risks
Support model clarity and ownership going forward
Status and resolution plan for all open tickets
Product roadmap visibility (to confirm long-term viability)
Data storage usage clarity
Pricing consistency vs prior invoices
Decisions Made
Introduce Desh → Jamie Alvarez (Account Manager)
Schedule product roadmap + renewal alignment meeting
Customer to share meeting notes
Customer to complete sustainability questionnaire (vendor categorization)
Agreed Next Steps
Immediate (Critical)
Introduce Jamie Alvarez to customer
Schedule joint call (AM + Product team) before June 21 deadline
Preparation Required (Internal)

Validate:
Usage + data storage levels
Renewal pricing vs historical invoices
Risk Assessment
Severity: High
Drivers: Trust erosion, support failure, no ownership
Recovery Path:
Strong, coordinated response from AM + Product
Immediate visibility + action on support backlog
Clear commitment to product continuity
Key Insight

This is not a pricing or product issue—this is a trust and ownership failure.
Recovery depends on demonstrating responsiveness, accountability, and continuity within the next interaction.
